text: Honestly, I wasn't expecting that — the whole thing just worked on the first try, which never happens.
annotated_text: [*Honestly*], I wasn't expecting that — [.] the whole thing just [~worked~] on the [*first try*], which [_never happens_].
direction_notes: Start with conviction on 'Honestly'. Brief pause after the dash — a moment of disbelief. Slight stress on 'worked'. Drop volume at the end for the dry understatement.

text: I mean, you'd think by now they'd have figured out a better way to handle those situations, right?
annotated_text: I mean, [.] you'd think by [*now*] they'd have figured out a better way to handle those situations, [^right^]?
direction_notes: Hesitation pause after 'I mean'. Stress 'now' with mild exasperation. Rising intonation on 'right?' — seeking agreement.

text: So I'm standing there, waiting for the bus, and this guy just walks up and starts singing opera.
annotated_text: So I'm standing there, waiting for the bus, [.] and this guy just walks up and starts [*singing opera*].
direction_notes: Storytelling register. Pause before the punchline. Emphasis on 'singing opera' — delivered with amused disbelief.

text: I've been meaning to tell you — I finally finished that book you recommended, and yeah, it was amazing.
annotated_text: I've been meaning to tell you — [.] I [*finally*] finished that book you recommended, [..] and yeah, it was [~amazing~].
direction_notes: Casual setup. Stress 'finally' — it took a while. Longer pause before the verdict. Moderate warmth on 'amazing'.

text: Look, I don't wanna sound negative, but I think we should've gone with the other option from the start.
annotated_text: [*Look*], [.] I don't wanna sound negative, but I think we should've gone with the [*other option*] from the [~start~].
direction_notes: Direct address — 'Look' gets firm emphasis. Brief pause to soften what follows. Stress 'other option' as the key point. Mild stress on 'start'.

text: It's not like I haven't tried — I've been working on this thing for, like, three weeks straight now.
annotated_text: It's not like I haven't [~tried~] — [.] I've been working on this thing for, like, [*three weeks straight*] now.
direction_notes: Defensive tone. Moderate stress on 'tried'. Pause after the dash. Emphasis on the duration 'three weeks straight' — conveying frustration.

text: You know what's funny — I actually thought about calling you right before you texted me out of nowhere.
annotated_text: You know what's [~funny~] — [.] I [*actually*] thought about calling you [.] right before you texted me [_out of nowhere_].
direction_notes: Light amusement on 'funny'. Pause for dramatic setup. Stress 'actually' as the coincidence. Drop at 'out of nowhere' — throwaway delivery.

text: I can't believe she didn't mention anything about it — I mean, we were literally just talking about that yesterday.
annotated_text: I [*can't believe*] she didn't mention anything about it — [.] I mean, we were [*literally*] just talking about that [~yesterday~].
direction_notes: Strong disbelief on 'can't believe'. Pause after the dash. Stress 'literally' for emphasis. Moderate stress on 'yesterday' — underscoring the recency.

text: Anyway, long story short, we ended up driving through the mountains instead of taking the highway.
annotated_text: [>fast]Anyway, long story short,[/fast] [.] we ended up driving through the [~mountains~] instead of taking the highway.
direction_notes: Rush through 'Anyway, long story short' — it's a verbal shortcut. Pause, then settle into normal pace. Gentle emphasis on 'mountains' — the unexpected choice.

text: I'm not gonna lie, I was pretty nervous at first, but once things got going it all felt natural.
annotated_text: I'm not gonna lie, [.] I was [~pretty nervous~] at first, [..] but once things got going it all felt [*natural*].
direction_notes: Honest admission. Pause after the preface. Moderate emphasis on 'pretty nervous' — vulnerability. Longer pause as the mood shifts. Strong positive emphasis on 'natural' — relief.
